item.title: 【Scaling AI Workloads in Java Without Breaking Your APIs】在不破坏 API 的情况下扩展 Java 中的 AI 工作负载
item.description:
【

As AI inference moves from prototype to production, Java services must handle high-concurrency workloads without disrupting existing APIs. This article examines patterns for scaling AI model serving in Java while preserving API contracts. Here, we compare synchronous and asynchronous approaches, including modern virtual threads and reactive streams, and discuss when to use in-process JNI/FFM calls versus network calls, gRPC/REST. We also present concrete guidelines for API versioning, timeouts, circuit breakers, bulkheads, rate limiting, graceful degradation, and observability using tools like Resilience4j, Micrometer, and OpenTelemetry. 

Detailed Java code examples illustrate each pattern from a blocking wrapper with a thread pool and queue to a non-blocking implementation using CompletableFuture and virtual threads to a Reactor-based example. We also show a gRPC client/server stub, a batching implementation, Resilience4j integration, and Micrometer/OpenTelemetry instrumentation, as well as performance considerations and deployment best practices. Finally, we offer a benchmarking strategy and a migration checklist with anti-patterns to avoid.

】

随着人工智能推理从原型转向生产，Java 服务必须在不中断现有 API 的情况下处理高并发工作负载。本文研究了在保留 API 契约的同时扩展 Java 中的 AI 模型服务的模式。在这里，我们比较同步和异步方法，包括现代虚拟线程和反应流，并讨论何时使用进程内 JNI/FFM 调用与网络调用、gRPC/REST。我们还提供了有关 API 版本控制、超时、断路器、舱壁、速率限制、优雅降级以及使用 Resilience4j、Micrometer 和 OpenTelemetry 等工具的可观察性的具体指南。 

详细的 Java 代码示例说明了每种模式，从带有线程池和队列的阻塞包装器到使用 CompletableFuture 和虚拟线程的非阻塞实现，再到基于 Reactor 的示例。我们还展示了 gRPC 客户端/服务器存根、批处理实现、Resilience4j 集成和 Micrometer/OpenTelemetry 工具，以及性能注意事项和部署最佳实践。最后，我们提供了基准测试策略和迁移清单，其中包含要避免的反模式。

item.title: 【The Hidden Cost of Legacy Infrastructure in Asset-Heavy Game Development】重资产游戏开发中遗留基础设施的隐性成本
item.description:
【

Game developers spend endless engineering hours optimizing shaders, draw calls, and memory footprints. Solutions for runtime performance have advanced dramatically over the last decade, but the production pipeline hasn't kept pace.

While engines like UE5 have revolutionized what we see on screen, the “pipes”, the version control systems (VCS) we use, have remained virtually static for over 20 years. This has created a pipeline performance plateau.

Today, the most critical bottleneck for a studio is no longer at the runtime level of the CPU or GPU; it is in the development pipeline through the operational drag of legacy version control.

The Obvious Choice is Git - or Is It?

For a long time, the consensus was that for standard software engineering, Git is THE go-to tool. Its decentralized nature and branch-based workflows changed how we work and enabled parallel development. But the narrative is shifting. As we move into the agentic era, Git’s decentralized architecture is becoming a fundamental bottleneck for everyone, not just game developers. 

】

游戏开发人员花费无尽的工程时间来优化着色器、绘制调用和内存占用。运行时性能解决方案在过去十年中取得了巨大进步，但生产流程却没有跟上步伐。

虽然像 UE5 这样的引擎彻底改变了我们在屏幕上看到的内容，但我们使用的“管道”、版本控制系统 (VCS) 20 多年来几乎保持不变。这造成了管道性能的稳定。

如今，工作室最关键的瓶颈不再是CPU或GPU的运行时级别，而是CPU或GPU的运行时级别。它通过旧版本控制的操作拖累进入开发流程。

显而易见的选择是 Git - 或者是吗？

长期以来，人们一致认为，对于标准软件工程来说，Git 是首选工具。它的去中心化性质和基于分支的工作流程改变了我们的工作方式并实现了并行开发。但叙事正在发生变化。随着我们进入代理时代，Git 的去中心化架构正在成为每个人的基本瓶颈，而不仅仅是游戏开发者。

item.title: 【The Hidden Cost of Flaky Tests in Test Automation】测试自动化中片状测试的隐性成本
item.description:
【

A test result on the CI pipeline fails. A developer runs the process again, and the test passes. No changes to the code had been made. This happens frequently enough that many teams have experienced it before. When this occurs, it is treated as a common occurrence, and after a while, it becomes routine. As a result, automatic builds get rerun on failure, and only if they fail again do they receive any follow-up attention. Ultimately, the CI pipeline cannot be relied upon as a safe environment; instead, it becomes ambient background noise.

Flaky tests are not just a nuisance. When flaky tests become frequent occurrences in CI processes, they undermine the team’s confidence, create inefficiencies within the development team, and introduce hidden costs that typically go unaccounted for.

】

CI 管道的测试结果失败。开发人员再次运行该过程，测试通过。代码未进行任何更改。这种情况发生的频率足够高，许多团队以前都经历过。当这种情况发生时，它被视为常见现象，过了一段时间，它就变成了例行公事。因此，自动构建会在失败时重新运行，并且只有当它们再次失败时才会收到任何后续关注。最终，CI 管道不能被视为安全的环境；相反，它变成了环境背景噪音。

片状测试不仅仅是一个麻烦。当不稳定的测试在 CI 流程中频繁出现时，它们会削弱团队的信心，导致开发团队效率低下，并引入通常无法计算的隐藏成本。

item.title: 【Designing High-Concurrency Databricks Workloads Without Performance Degradation】设计高并发 Databricks 工作负载而不降低性能
item.description:
【

High concurrency in Databricks means many jobs or queries running in parallel, accessing the same data. Delta Lake provides ACID transactions and snapshot isolation, but without care, concurrent writes can conflict and waste compute. 

Optimizing the Delta table layout and Databricks' settings lets engineers keep performance stable under load. Key strategies include:

】

Databricks 中的高并发意味着许多作业或查询并行运行，访问相同的数据。 Delta Lake 提供 ACID 事务和快照隔离，但如果不小心，并发写入可能会发生冲突并浪费计算。 

优化 Delta 表布局和 Databricks 设置可让工程师在负载下保持性能稳定。主要策略包括：

item.title: 【Why Good Models Fail After Deployment】为什么好的模型在部署后会失败
item.description:
【

Six months ago, your recommendation model looked perfect. It hit 95% accuracy on the test set, passed cross-validation with strong scores, and the A/B test showed a 3% lift in engagement. The team celebrated and deployed with confidence.

Today, that model is failing. Click-through rates have declined steadily. Users are complaining. The monitoring dashboards show no errors or crashes, but something has broken. The model that performed so well during development is struggling in production, and the decline was unexpected.

】

六个月前，您的推荐模型看起来很完美。它在测试集上的准确率达到了 95%，通过了交叉验证并获得了很高的分数，A/B 测试显示参与度提高了 3%。团队充满信心地庆祝和部署。

如今，该模式正在失败。点击率稳步下降。用户正在抱怨。监控仪表板显示没有错误或崩溃，但有些东西坏了。在开发过程中表现出色的模型在生产中却举步维艰，下滑令人始料未及。

item.title: 【The Self-Healing Endpoint: Why Automation Alone No Longer Cuts It】自我修复端点：为什么仅靠自动化不再能解决问题
item.description:
【

Most organizations have poured heavy capital into endpoint automation. That investment has yielded partial results at best. IT teams frequently find themselves trapped maintaining the very scripts designed to save them time. 

Recent data from the Automox 2026 State of Endpoint Management report reveals that only 6% of organizations consider themselves fully automated. Meanwhile, 57% operate as partially automated using custom workflows. 

】

大多数组织都在端点自动化方面投入了大量资金。这项投资充其量只取得了部分成果。 IT 团队经常发现自己陷入了维护旨在节省时间的脚本的困境。 

Automox 2026 年端点管理状况报告的最新数据显示，只有 6% 的组织认为自己完全自动化。与此同时，57% 使用自定义工作流程进行部分自动化操作。

item.title: 【Engineering High-Performance Real-Time Leaderboard】工程高性能实时排行榜
item.description:
【

Leaderboard performance problems rarely announce themselves as “data structure issues.” They surface instead as CPU spikes, tail-latency explosions, and on-call alerts that refuse to quiet down. That’s exactly what we encountered: a slice-based leaderboard implementation that initially appeared perfectly reasonable, but began to collapse once the system surpassed the 10,000-user mark and update workloads started behaving like O(N²).

This article walks through what broke, how profiling made the root cause undeniable, and how the issue was fixed by replacing the original approach with an indexed skip list augmented with span counters and a hash-based identity layer. The redesign reduced critical operations to O(log N), stabilized memory usage, and pushed update latency below one millisecond.

】

排行榜性能问题很少以“数据结构问题”的形式出现。相反，它们会随着 CPU 峰值、尾部延迟爆炸以及无法安静下来的待命警报而出现。这正是我们遇到的情况：基于分片的排行榜实现最初看起来完全合理，但一旦系统超过 10,000 个用户大关，并且更新工作负载开始表现得像 O(N²)，就开始崩溃。

本文将介绍问题所在、分析如何使根本原因变得不可否认，以及如何通过用跨度计数器和基于哈希的身份层增强的索引跳跃列表替换原始方法来解决问题。重新设计将关键操作减少到 O(log N)，稳定了内存使用，并将更新延迟降至一毫秒以下。

item.title: 【Essential Techniques for Production Vector Search Systems, Part 5: Reranking】生产向量搜索系统的基本技术，第 5 部分：重新排名
item.description:
【

After implementing vector search systems at multiple companies, I wanted to document efficient techniques that can be very helpful for successful production deployments.

I want to present these techniques by showcasing when to apply each one, how they complement each other, and the trade-offs they introduce. This will be a multi-part series that introduces all of the techniques one by one in each article. I have also included code snippets to quickly test each technique.

】

在多家公司实施矢量搜索系统后，我想记录对成功生产部署非常有帮助的高效技术。

我想通过展示何时应用每种技术、它们如何相互补充以及它们引入的权衡来展示这些技术。这将是一个由多部分组成的系列，每篇文章都会一一介绍所有技术。我还提供了代码片段来快速测试每种技术。

item.title: 【Designing Stop Loss in Modern AI-Driven Automated Trading Systems】现代人工智能驱动的自动交易系统中的止损设计
item.description:
【

From Rule-Based Algos to AI-Based Decision Systems

A decade ago, many electronic trading strategies were still mostly rule-based. You could often explain the logic in a few sentences. The systems were automated, but the decision rules were transparent and easy for humans to reason about.

Modern quantitative desks increasingly lean on machine learning and deep learning — and if we want to be a bit buzzwordy, we can call these AI-based trading systems. Models ingest high-dimensional order book data, news, and alternative data, and decisions are made by gradient-boosted trees, deep networks, or ensembles rather than hand-coded heuristics.

】

从基于规则的算法到基于人工智能的决策系统

十年前，许多电子交易策略仍然主要基于规则。你通常可以用几句话解释其中的逻辑。系统是自动化的，但决策规则是透明的并且易于人类推理。

现代定量分析台越来越依赖于机器学习和深度学习——如果我们想用点流行语，我们可以称这些为基于人工智能的交易系统。模型摄取高维订单簿数据、新闻和替代数据，并通过梯度增强树、深度网络或集成做出决策，而不是通过手动编码的启发式方法做出决策。

item.title: 【Stop Leap-Second AI Drift in IoT Streams With PySpark】使用 PySpark 阻止物联网流中的闰秒 AI 漂移
item.description:
【

Fintech and Enterprise platforms ingest massive volumes of timestamped data (big data) from IoT devices such as payment terminals, wearables, and mobile apps. Accurate timing is essential for fraud detection, risk scoring, and customer analytics. Yet a subtle irregularity called the leap second can corrupt timestamps and trigger AI drift, gradually degrading model performance in production. 

In this article, I will attempt to explain clearly what drift types are and how they can be prevented, based on my research paper. Details can be found here. Let's start.

】

金融科技和企业平台从支付终端、可穿戴设备和移动应用等物联网设备中获取大量带时间戳的数据（大数据）。准确的计时对于欺诈检测、风险评分和客户分析至关重要。然而，一种称为闰秒的微妙不规则性可能会破坏时间戳并引发人工智能漂移，从而逐渐降低生产中的模型性能。 

在本文中，我将根据我的研究论文，尝试清楚地解释什么是漂移类型以及如何预防它们。详情请参阅此处。让我们开始吧。
